recommendations: stabilize packaging metadata detection (tool reports missing `pyproject/setup.py` despite files existing) by fixing repository root/path resolution in the analyzer
add a minimal smoke-test suite for generated MCP endpoints (import + single call per endpoint) to replace empty test status
de-duplicate/conflict-proof endpoint names (e.g.
repeated `event`
class/function name collisions like `trace`/`Trace`) with explicit namespacing
introduce dependency-aware endpoint gating so optional-heavy modules (Qt/VTK/cartopy/obspy) are only registered when installed
add contract validation for endpoint signatures and docstrings to avoid exposing low-level/internal APIs unintentionally
generate focused integration tests for high-value workflows (`trace`
`cake`
`gf`
`squirrel`) using small deterministic fixtures
improve error handling and observability in `mcp_service.py` (structured logs
timeout/retry for network-backed calls
typed error mapping)
add performance baselines for expensive paths (`gf.seismosizer`
large `squirrel` queries) and cache/reuse heavy model loads
harden security by restricting file/network-capable endpoints and validating paths/URLs
improve README_MCP with executable quickstart + dependency matrix + known limitations
set up CI for plugin output (`ruff/black`
type checks
pytest
import matrix across Python versions)
and add a compatibility/versioning policy to track upstream Pyrocko changes and automatically detect broken adapters on update

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Robust fallback behavior: after git clone failure, pipeline continued via zip fallback and processed repository content (~495 files).
MCP plugin scaffold was generated successfully with runnable entrypoint and healthy stdio service status.
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for Pyrocko’s Python-first architecture (import feasibility 0.84).
failure_reasons: Primary repository acquisition failed due to transient/transport-level Git HTTP2 RPC error (curl 16, framing layer).
Analyzer/path-resolution inconsistency caused false negatives in dependency metadata detection (pyproject/setup.py reported missing despite presence).
Quality signals are weakened by missing/zeroed metrics (llm_calls, files_processed, LOC, performance metrics), indicating telemetry/instrumentation gaps rather than pure execution failure.
Generated endpoint catalog has naming collisions/duplication risk (e.g., repeated `event`, class/function name overlaps).

node_performance.download_time: Download node partially degraded: clone failed, fallback succeeded. Likely a notable share of total 311.6s due to retry/fallback overhead.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed and identified core modules/CLI/dependency profile, but structured analyzer outputs were inconsistent (empty packages/entry points).
node_performance.generation_time: Generation succeeded quickly enough to produce full scaffold; however generated metadata reports 0 LOC/0 file size, suggesting post-generation measurement failure.
node_performance.test_time: Service smoke health passed, but original project tests did not run meaningfully (passed=false with empty details, execution_time=0). Effective validation depth is low.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/unevaluable: reported as 0 MB due to missing instrumentation.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/unevaluable: reported as 0% due to missing instrumentation.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Repository and generated files clearly exist, but reported generated size is 0; disk accounting is unreliable.
